title: Congee with Century Egg

seo.title: How to Make Congee with Century Egg — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Congee with Century Egg. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Congee with Century Egg
easy Congee with Century Egg recipe
authentic Chinese Congee with Century Egg
lowCompetitionKeywords: Chinese Congee with Century Egg recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Congee with Century Egg Recipe
h2Headings: What is Congee with Century Egg?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Congee with Century Egg recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Congee with Century Egg — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Congee with Century Egg is a comforting and savory Chinese breakfast staple, often enjoyed for its rich flavor and smooth texture. This traditional porridge combines tender rice with the unique, umami taste of century eggs and often includes shredded pork and aromatic ginger. It is a deeply satisfying and nourishing dish perfect for a chilly morning or a light meal.

ingredients.notes: peeled and cut into small wedges
peeled and thinly sliced or julienned

instructions.title: Prepare the Rice and Pork
instructions.text: Rinse the jasmine rice thoroughly until the water runs clear. Soak the rice in cold water for at least 30 minutes, or overnight for an even creamier congee. While the rice soaks, prepare the pork by shredding or dicing it finely.

instructions.title: Start Cooking the Congee Base
instructions.text: Drain the soaked rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, combine the drained rice with 8-10 cups of water or chicken broth. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then immediately reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot, leaving a small gap for steam to escape, and simmer for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Prepare Century Eggs and Ginger
instructions.text: While the congee simmers, peel the century eggs carefully and cut them into small, bite-sized wedges. Peel and thinly slice or julienne the fresh ginger.

instructions.title: Add Pork and Century Eggs
instructions.text: After 25 minutes, the rice should have broken down and the congee will be thickening. Add the shredded pork, century egg wedges, and most of the sliced ginger to the pot. Stir well to combine.

instructions.title: Continue Simmering and Season
instructions.text: Continue to simmer the congee for another 7-10 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and the congee reaches your desired consistency. It should be thick and creamy. Season with salt and white pepper to taste. If the congee becomes too thick, you can add a little more hot water or broth.

instructions.title: Serve Hot with Garnishes
instructions.text: Ladle the hot congee into individual serving bowls. Drizzle each serving with a touch of sesame oil and garnish generously with chopped green onions and the remaining fresh ginger. Serve immediately and enjoy this authentic Chinese breakfast.

tips: Soaking the rice beforehand helps it break down faster and results in a smoother, creamier congee texture. For an extra creamy congee, freeze the soaked rice overnight before cooking.
For a richer flavor, use homemade chicken broth instead of water. If you are not a fan of pork, you can substitute it with shredded chicken or omit it entirely for a simpler version.

Congee is best served immediately. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days, but the consistency may thicken. Reheat gently with a splash of water or broth.
article: Congee with Century Egg is a beloved Chinese Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
